steps.explanation: Identify the variables: h = hours at regular job, c = hours at second job. The equation 20h + 14c = 716 expresses total weekly earnings in dollars.
In the term 20h, the units of h are hours and the product 20h must be in dollars. So 20 has units of dollars per hour — it is the hourly rate for the regular job.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her regular job. The coefficient of h (regular-job hours) is 20, representing \$20 per hour at the regular job. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: The amount per hour at the second job. The second-job rate is 14 (the coefficient of c), not 20. Eliminated.
  • C: The number of hours worked at the regular job. The number of hours at the regular job is h, the variable, not the constant 20. Eliminated.
  • D: The number of hours worked at the second job. The number of hours at the second job is c. Eliminated.
